title: Intraoperative recordings of medianus stimulation with low and high impedance ECoG
description: This dataset comprises intraoperative intracranial EEG recordings from 10 patients undergoing brain tumor resection in the perirolandic region. Median nerve somatosensory evoked potentials (SEP) were recorded simultaneously using two 4-contact ECoG electrode strips with different impedance characteristics (low impedance: 3.4 kΩ and high impedance: 6.9 kΩ) to investigate the efficacy of low-impedance electrodes for detecting high-frequency oscillations in clinical neurophysiology.
methods_description: Intraoperative median nerve stimulation at 4.7 Hz was recorded from two simultaneous 4-contact ECoG strips with different impedance profiles. Raw continuous ECoG data were acquired and processed offline using custom MATLAB scripts. Data analysis included high-pass filtering (200 Hz cutoff), stimulus artifact detection via local peak detection, sweep definition (50 ms post-stimulus), artifact rejection (±100 µV amplitude threshold), and averaging of 100 sweeps. Bandpass filtering was applied at [30-300] Hz for N20 analysis, [250-500] Hz for fast ripple detection, and [500-1000] Hz for high-frequency oscillation analysis.
